response:
Sub-problem 1 asks for the probability of exactly 15 rooms needing attention when the mean is 20 under a Poisson distribution. The formula uses e to the power of negative 20 multiplied by 20 to the power of 15 divided by 15 factorial. This calculation yields approximately 0.0347.

Sub-problem 2 requires finding the probability that at least 7 out of 8 concerns are resolved with a 90% success rate using the binomial distribution. This means calculating the sum of probabilities for exactly 7 successes and exactly 8 successes. The result for 7 successes is 8 times 0.9 to the power of 7 times 0.1 to the power of 1. The result for 8 successes is 0.9 to the power of 8. Adding these two values gives a final probability of approximately 0.9560.
